footerDescription: Stay updated for workshops, jam sessions, and ticket releases.
travelDescription:
By Public Transport

The nearest train station is Bahnhof Fehring. There are direct train connections from Vienna and Graz to Fehring. From the station, the venue is approximately 30 minutes on foot or around 10 minutes by bike. Taxis are also available in the area.

We recommend checking ÖBB schedules in advance and planning your arrival ahead of time, especially for late evening connections.

By Car

If you are arriving by car, please use:
Kasernenstraße 2, 8350 Fehring, Austria

Please follow festival signage and staff directions near the venue. We encourage carpooling whenever possible and ask all guests to drive respectfully within the local area.

Important

Please organize your return journey in advance, especially if leaving during the night or early morning hours.
faqDescription: Frequently asked questions about the festival experience.
supportDescription: If you would like to support us, every donation helps us keep participation accessible and inclusive. Your contribution directly supports safer spaces, workshop materials, and community-led programming.
venueDescription: An intimate open-air venue surrounded by nature — featuring forest stages, chill areas, camping zones, and immersive spaces built for juggling
partnersDescription: Supporters and collaborators of the festival.
note: Ticket sales are managed on Pretix.
heroLead:
FLINTA+ is an inclusive umbrella term used for women, lesbians, intersex, non-binary, trans, and agender people. 

Kunterbunt Juggling Festival is built as a safer and joyful space where this community and allies can practice, learn, and celebrate together. 

During three vibrant days, participants share tricks in the open hall, join workshops, party in renegade, and enjoy shows designed around solidarity and creativity.
travelBullets: A dedicated camper area will be available on-site for guests arriving with vans or campers. Please note that no electricity or power hook-ups will be provided in the camper area. Come prepared for a fully self-sufficient stay.

faqs.question: What are the accommodation options?
faqs.answer:
Guests can stay in one of the following accommodation areas:

Dorm rooms
Tent area
Camper area

Showers and sanitary facilities are located close to all accommodation areas and will be accessible throughout the festival.

Please note that the rooms are shared dorm-style accommodations. The tent and camper areas may also remain active and noisy during the night. We recommend bringing earplugs if you are sensitive to sound while sleeping.

The camper area does not include electricity or power hook-ups, so please come prepared for a self-sufficient stay.

faqs.question: Food & drink options
faqs.answer:
Food & Drink Options

A nourishing breakfast is included in every festival ticket.

Vegetarian lunch options will be available on-site for approximately €10 per meal.

For guests who prefer to prepare their own food, a fully functional shared kitchen will also be accessible throughout the festival.
